dismissalText.commentary: Sai Sudharsan c Hinge b Sakib Hussain 61 (44b 5x4 2x6 86m) SR: 138.63

dismissalComment.html: Sudharsan tries to be funky, and pays the price! Well, to be fair to him, GT needed an injection of momentum. Yorker on leg and Sudharsan tries to reverse-lap it. Does not get into a good enough position (neither low enough, nor with the requisite room), and he plonks it to short third. Hinge with a slightly unconventional catching technique but he holds on!

dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill c Klaasen b Hinge 5 (7b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 71.42

dismissalComment.html: has that carried?! Looks like it has, and Hinge has sent the GT skipper packing! Full and slanting into off stump. Gill plants his front foot and looks to shovel an on-drive away. Does not get on top of it, and does not get under it enough either. Hits it flat to Klaasen at mid on, who lunges forward and picks it up inches off the turf. GT rocked early!

dismissalText.commentary: Jos Buttler c †Ishan Kishan b Hinge 7 (11b 1x4 0x6 14m) SR: 63.63

dismissalComment.html: appeal for a caught-behind, but the umpire responds with a wide call! And SRH review. Full and sneaking a touch down leg. Buttler shapes for the scoop and shuffles across, and Kishan and the bowler think there is bat involved. And they are fully correct. Clear spike when the ball is next to the bat, and Buttler has to go!

dismissalText.commentary: Nishant Sindhu c Klaasen b Cummins 22 (14b 3x4 1x6 24m) SR: 157.14

dismissalComment.html: launched straight to long off, and Sindhu has to trudge off! Fullish outside off and this is there to be lofted. Sindhu, though, tries to take on the sweeper and on this sluggish track, that is fraught with danger. Chips it straight down Klaasen's throat, and Sindhu has to walk back after a sprightly cameo!

dismissalText.commentary: Washington Sundar c †Ishan Kishan b Sakib Hussain 50 (33b 7x4 1x6 56m) SR: 151.51

dismissalComment.html: skewed a mile up, and Kishan lunges forward to gobble it up! What a grab! Off-pace and into the track on middle and off. Sundar does not pick the variation and is a week early into the stroke. Swipes at it anyway and the ball goes straight up. Kishan charges after it and dives forward to complete the catch in the square leg region. Top effort, but take nothing away from a top Sundar knock either!

dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill c Klaasen b Hinge 5 (7b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 71.42

dismissalComment.html: has that carried?! Looks like it has, and Hinge has sent the GT skipper packing! Full and slanting into off stump. Gill plants his front foot and looks to shovel an on-drive away. Does not get on top of it, and does not get under it enough either. Hits it flat to Klaasen at mid on, who lunges forward and picks it up inches off the turf. GT rocked early!

dismissalComment.html: appeal for a caught-behind, but the umpire responds with a wide call! And SRH review. Full and sneaking a touch down leg. Buttler shapes for the scoop and shuffles across, and Kishan and the bowler think there is bat involved. And they are fully correct. Clear spike when the ball is next to the bat, and Buttler has to go!

dismissalText.commentary: Nishant Sindhu c Klaasen b Cummins 22 (14b 3x4 1x6 24m) SR: 157.14

dismissalComment.html: launched straight to long off, and Sindhu has to trudge off! Fullish outside off and this is there to be lofted. Sindhu, though, tries to take on the sweeper and on this sluggish track, that is fraught with danger. Chips it straight down Klaasen's throat, and Sindhu has to walk back after a sprightly cameo!

dismissalText.commentary: Sai Sudharsan c Hinge b Sakib Hussain 61 (44b 5x4 2x6 86m) SR: 138.63

dismissalComment.html: Sudharsan tries to be funky, and pays the price! Well, to be fair to him, GT needed an injection of momentum. Yorker on leg and Sudharsan tries to reverse-lap it. Does not get into a good enough position (neither low enough, nor with the requisite room), and he plonks it to short third. Hinge with a slightly unconventional catching technique but he holds on!

dismissalText.commentary: Washington Sundar c †Ishan Kishan b Sakib Hussain 50 (33b 7x4 1x6 56m) SR: 151.51

dismissalComment.html: skewed a mile up, and Kishan lunges forward to gobble it up! What a grab! Off-pace and into the track on middle and off. Sundar does not pick the variation and is a week early into the stroke. Swipes at it anyway and the ball goes straight up. Kishan charges after it and dives forward to complete the catch in the square leg region. Top effort, but take nothing away from a top Sundar knock either!

dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c Sindhu b Mohammed Siraj 0 (4b 0x4 0x6) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: off the leading edge, and caught at deep backward point! Dream over by Siraj, and now the dream start for GT! Siraj has had Head on toast throughout the over, and now he has his reward. Back of a length on leg. Shapes away and Head gets turned inside out. Looks to flick it away but all he does is find the sweeper via the leading edge. Siraj thrilled to bits, and this crowd has come alive!

dismissalText.commentary: Abhishek Sharma b Rabada 6 (4b 0x4 1x6) SR: 150

dismissalComment.html: chopped on in the most freakish of manners, and Rabada roars back! Shortish and following the batter as he steps outside leg. Abhishek looks to defend but the extra pace means that he only manages to dab it onto his stumps. What a moment. Abhishek cannot believe it. But the crowd believes now. SRH rocked twice in two overs, and this game is turning!

dismissalText.commentary: Heinrich Klaasen c †Buttler b Holder 14 (16b 2x4 0x6) SR: 87.5

dismissalComment.html: a massive top edge, and Klaasen perishes too! And is that the game?! Back of a length on off and this seems to stick in the surface. Klaasen looks to tug it leg-side but the extra bounce, plus the lack of pace, means the ball loops up off the leading/top edge. Buttler calls early, runs back to his left and tumbles to complete the catch. Holder is overjoyed, and this might be the final nail in SRH's coffin for the night!

dismissalText.commentary: Nitish Kumar Reddy c Washington Sundar b Holder 2 (4b 0x4 0x6) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: sliced to backward point, and catch Holder if you can now! GT on the prowl, and SRH sliding! Back of a length outside off and this kicks up just a shade. Nitish tries to punch it through the off side but gets it too much behind point, which brings Sundar, who is in very tight, into play at backward point. He moves smartly to his left and does the rest, and GT have SRH seven down inside 11 overs!

dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c Sindhu b Mohammed Siraj 0 (4b 0x4 0x6) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: off the leading edge, and caught at deep backward point! Dream over by Siraj, and now the dream start for GT! Siraj has had Head on toast throughout the over, and now he has his reward. Back of a length on leg. Shapes away and Head gets turned inside out. Looks to flick it away but all he does is find the sweeper via the leading edge. Siraj thrilled to bits, and this crowd has come alive!

dismissalText.commentary: Abhishek Sharma b Rabada 6 (4b 0x4 1x6) SR: 150

dismissalComment.html: chopped on in the most freakish of manners, and Rabada roars back! Shortish and following the batter as he steps outside leg. Abhishek looks to defend but the extra pace means that he only manages to dab it onto his stumps. What a moment. Abhishek cannot believe it. But the crowd believes now. SRH rocked twice in two overs, and this game is turning!

dismissalText.commentary: Heinrich Klaasen c †Buttler b Holder 14 (16b 2x4 0x6) SR: 87.5

dismissalComment.html: a massive top edge, and Klaasen perishes too! And is that the game?! Back of a length on off and this seems to stick in the surface. Klaasen looks to tug it leg-side but the extra bounce, plus the lack of pace, means the ball loops up off the leading/top edge. Buttler calls early, runs back to his left and tumbles to complete the catch. Holder is overjoyed, and this might be the final nail in SRH's coffin for the night!

dismissalText.commentary: Nitish Kumar Reddy c Washington Sundar b Holder 2 (4b 0x4 0x6) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: sliced to backward point, and catch Holder if you can now! GT on the prowl, and SRH sliding! Back of a length outside off and this kicks up just a shade. Nitish tries to punch it through the off side but gets it too much behind point, which brings Sundar, who is in very tight, into play at backward point. He moves smartly to his left and does the rest, and GT have SRH seven down inside 11 overs!
